article.title: FDA Grants Accelerated Approval to Sonrotoclax for Relapsed Mantle Cell Lymphoma With 52% Response Rate in BGB-11417-201
article.summary: BeOne Medicines' Beqalzi clears FDA as the first and only BCL2 inhibitor approved for relapsed/refractory mantle cell lymphoma after a BTK inhibitor, on a single-arm 103-patient Phase 1/2 trial.
article.body_markdown:
## Overview

The U.S. Food and Drug Administration granted accelerated approval on May 13, 2026 to sonrotoclax, a BCL-2 inhibitor from BeOne Medicines that will be marketed under the brand name Beqalzi, for adults with relapsed or refractory mantle cell lymphoma after at least two lines of systemic therapy, including a Bruton's tyrosine kinase inhibitor, [BeOne Medicines announced in a press release distributed via Yahoo Finance](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/beone-medicines-beqalzi-sonrotoclax-approved-172700286.html). The same announcement described Beqalzi as the ["First and Only BCL2 Inhibitor for R/R Mantle Cell Lymphoma"](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/beone-medicines-beqalzi-sonrotoclax-approved-172700286.html). The [U.S. Food and Drug Administration](https://www.fda.gov/drugs/resources-information-approved-drugs/fda-grants-accelerated-approval-sonrotoclax-relapsed-or-refractory-mantle-cell-lymphoma) characterized the clearance as an accelerated approval, contingent on confirmatory evidence in a follow-on Phase 3 study.

## What We Know

### Trial design and patient population

The approval rests on BGB-11417-201 (NCT05471843), a single-arm, multicenter trial in 103 adults with relapsed or refractory mantle cell lymphoma who had previously received anti-CD20-based therapy and a BTK inhibitor, [according to the FDA](https://www.fda.gov/drugs/resources-information-approved-drugs/fda-grants-accelerated-approval-sonrotoclax-relapsed-or-refractory-mantle-cell-lymphoma). The trial was an open-label Phase 1/2 study. BeOne said data from the same study is also under review by the European Medicines Agency and other regulatory agencies, [the company noted in its press release](https://www.biospace.com/press-releases/beone-medicines-beqalzi-sonrotoclax-approved-by-u-s-fda-as-first-and-only-bcl2-inhibitor-for-r-r-mantle-cell-lymphoma).

### Efficacy outcomes

The overall response rate was 52% (95% CI, 42 to 62), with a median time to response of 1.9 months, [BeOne reported in its press release](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/beone-medicines-beqalzi-sonrotoclax-approved-172700286.html). The complete response rate was 16% (95% CI, 9.1 to 24.0), and the median duration of response reached 15.8 months (95% CI, 7.4 months to not estimable), [according to the press release distributed via BioSpace](https://www.biospace.com/press-releases/beone-medicines-beqalzi-sonrotoclax-approved-by-u-s-fda-as-first-and-only-bcl2-inhibitor-for-r-r-mantle-cell-lymphoma). The FDA noted that those response durability figures reflected an estimated median follow-up of 11.9 months, [the agency said](https://www.fda.gov/drugs/resources-information-approved-drugs/fda-grants-accelerated-approval-sonrotoclax-relapsed-or-refractory-mantle-cell-lymphoma).

### Dosing and safety

The label calls for a four-week ramp-up phase intended to reduce the risk of tumor lysis syndrome, followed by 320 mg taken orally once daily until disease progression or unacceptable toxicity, [the FDA announcement detailed](https://www.fda.gov/drugs/resources-information-approved-drugs/fda-grants-accelerated-approval-sonrotoclax-relapsed-or-refractory-mantle-cell-lymphoma). In the trial, laboratory or clinical tumor lysis syndrome occurred in 7% of patients who followed the recommended ramp-up, [BeOne reported](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/beone-medicines-beqalzi-sonrotoclax-approved-172700286.html).

### Regulatory pathway and confirmatory trial

The FDA granted the sonrotoclax application Breakthrough Therapy Designation, Fast Track Designation, and Orphan Drug Designation, [BeOne said in its release](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/beone-medicines-beqalzi-sonrotoclax-approved-172700286.html). The review was conducted under Project Orbis, an initiative of the FDA's Oncology Center of Excellence that allows concurrent submission and review of oncology drugs across international regulators, [the agency said](https://www.fda.gov/drugs/resources-information-approved-drugs/fda-grants-accelerated-approval-sonrotoclax-relapsed-or-refractory-mantle-cell-lymphoma).

Because the approval is an accelerated approval rather than a regular approval, continued marketing authorization depends on confirmation of clinical benefit in a follow-on study. That study, the Phase 3 CELESTIAL-RRMCL trial (NCT06742996), has a primary completion target of August 2028, [BioSpace reported](https://www.biospace.com/fda/beone-wins-mantle-cell-lymphoma-approval-opening-new-therapy-class).

### Company and investigator framing

Amit Agarwal, Chief Medical Officer of Hematology at BeOne Medicines, said in the [company's announcement](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/beone-medicines-beqalzi-sonrotoclax-approved-172700286.html) that "BeOne is leading the advancement and enhancement of BCL2 inhibition to revolutionize how we treat patients living with B-cell malignancies." Michael Wang, M.D., Global Principal Investigator and a faculty member in the Department of Lymphoma and Myeloma at The University of Texas MD Anderson Cancer Center, added in [the same announcement](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/beone-medicines-beqalzi-sonrotoclax-approved-172700286.html) that "The data supporting the approval of sonrotoclax confirm its role as a foundational therapy for mantle cell lymphoma."

BeOne also disclosed that more than 2,200 patients have been enrolled across the broader sonrotoclax global development program to date, [according to the press release](https://www.biospace.com/press-releases/beone-medicines-beqalzi-sonrotoclax-approved-by-u-s-fda-as-first-and-only-bcl2-inhibitor-for-r-r-mantle-cell-lymphoma).

## What We Don't Know

The accelerated approval pathway means Beqalzi enters the market on intermediate evidence rather than confirmed overall survival benefit. The CELESTIAL-RRMCL Phase 3 trial, intended to convert the conditional approval into a regular approval, is not scheduled to reach primary completion until August 2028, [per BioSpace](https://www.biospace.com/fda/beone-wins-mantle-cell-lymphoma-approval-opening-new-therapy-class), leaving roughly two years before the regulatory commitment is tested.

Commercial expectations in the indication are modest. Truist Securities analysts described the approval as "incremental," citing a "relatively small" relapsed/refractory mantle cell lymphoma market with forecasted peak sales of "$300 to $400 million" for this indication, [BioSpace reported](https://www.biospace.com/fda/beone-wins-mantle-cell-lymphoma-approval-opening-new-therapy-class). The label restricts use to patients who have already failed at least two prior systemic regimens, which limits the addressable population.

The trial that supported approval was single-arm and uncontrolled, so the 52% response rate cannot be directly compared with outcomes on alternative therapies in the same line of treatment without head-to-head data. The confirmatory study is intended to address that gap.

## Analysis

Mantle cell lymphoma has had a steady drumbeat of second- and third-line approvals over the past several years, mostly built around BTK inhibitors and CAR-T cells. Sonrotoclax opens a different mechanism for patients whose disease has already progressed through a BTK inhibitor, which is now part of standard care. The 52% response rate and 15.8-month median duration of response — the durability figure in particular — give clinicians a measurable single-agent option to slot in after BTK failure, a setting where outcomes have historically been poor.

For BeOne Medicines, the approval anchors a B-cell malignancy franchise that the company has been building around its in-house pipeline. The conditional nature of the approval and the Phase 3 timeline mean the program's commercial trajectory will be shaped not only by uptake but by whether CELESTIAL-RRMCL delivers a confirmatory readout in 2028.
